mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly increasing present values of future equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling aggregate demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which provides immediate positive earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of high inflation or supply chain bottlenecks, or 5-day returns are negative in > 55% of rising INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5 percent while the unemployment rate remains stable or declines, accommodative monetary policy successfully offsets labor market slack, boosting risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns or shows no directional edge when unemployment rises concurrently, or the interaction effect is nullified by rising inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal fails to predict direction when GDP growth is simultaneously strong.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding employment and rising household income, which supports near-term consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll signal shows no predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of positive payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces retail sales growth, manufacturing capacity expansion and inventory accumulation signal robust corporate balance sheets and pricing power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction when inventory-to-sales ratios exceed historical averages, or the interaction yields negative predictive power during demand shocks.
